creator: tri@FreeBSD.org
text:
Since 1.1.1999 11 so called EMU countries within the European 
Union have had constant exchange rate against the upcoming Euro
currency.  In effect this means that exchange rates between these 
countries do not change and rate against other currencies have to
be defined only against Euro.  For example rate between US-dollar
and Finnish-markka is useless, if rate between US-dollar and euro
is known.

Fix: Here is the patch to units.lib file.  It defines EMU-currencies 
against Euro (this doesn't change) and Euro against dollar (this 
changes, but do does other rates too).  Patch is here as base64 
first (to spare tabs and spaces) and in cut/paste ascii later 
(for reference).
